title: garlicky spring syrian lamb tagine
description: a flavorful and aromatic lamb tagine infused with the warmth of madras paste and the sweetness of a glaze, perfect for a festive spring dinner
instructions: heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
add the madras paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the lamb is tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
while the lamb is cooking, prepare the glaze by whisking together the honey and lemon juice in a small bowl.
after the lamb has cooked for 1 hour, brush the glaze over the top of the lamb and continue to cook for another 30 minutes.
remove the lamb from the heat and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving.
garnish with chopped parsley and mint, if desired.
